narrative:
On 19 May 2026, the Karnataka High Court heard 319 of 876 scheduled cases, yielding an overall hearing rate of **36.4%** — marginally above the 35.0% recorded on 27 April but below the 42.0% seen on 25 April, suggesting performance remains within a narrow, middling range across recent sittings.

**Judge-wise**, the day was sharply polarised. Hall 10 under **C.M. Poonacha** delivered the strongest output: an 79.2% hearing rate across 125 cases in one session and 41.0% across a separate 105-case docket. **K. V. Aravind** (Hall 11) cleared 63.4% of 194 cases, and **Suraj Govindaraj & K. Manmadha Rao** (Hall 9) maintained a consistent 66.7% across case types. At the other extreme, **S.G. Pandit & K. V. Aravind** in Hall 5 heard only 13 of 386 listed cases (3.4%), drastically suppressing the court-wide average. Hall 22 under **S. Rachaiah** recorded zero hearings across four scheduled cases.

**By stage**, the ORDERS queue was the most productive at 70.8% (126/178), and ADMISSION cases converted at 64.5%. In stark contrast, FURTHER HEARING — the largest docket at 364 cases — saw a near-total stall at just 0.8% conversion, a pattern consistent with historical data where this stage consistently underperforms.

**By case type**, Writ Appeals (WA) again dominated scheduling at 397 cases but cleared only 5.0%, consistent with the 6.0% and 5.2% rates observed in late April. Writ Petitions (WP) and Criminal Petitions (CRL.P) performed steadily at 61.5% and 63.6% respectively — both notably higher than their April benchmarks of ~36–39%. COMAP and RFA dockets achieved full clearance, though their volumes were small.
